questions.q: The SAARC region includes all the countries of South Asia except
Which of the following SAARC initiatives aims at improving the economic and social conditions of women?
Describe the role of SAARC in promoting regional cooperation in South Asia. (10 marks)
questions.answer: Role of SAARC is to promote regional cooperation through various mechanisms such as summits, councils, and committees.

questions.q: State any two major challenges faced by SAARC in implementing its decisions. (4 marks)
questions.answer: Major challenges faced by SAARC include lack of trust among member states and limited institutional capacity.

questions.q: The SAARC region has a number of major river systems. Discuss the impact of these river systems on the economy of the region. (15 marks)
questions.answer: Major river systems in SAARC region have significant impact on economy through irrigation, transportation, and hydroelectric power generation.

questions.q: Which SAARC member state is not a part of the South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation (SAARC) region?
What is the SAARC Charter? Describe its significance in the context of regional cooperation in South Asia. (8 marks)
questions.answer: The SAARC Charter is a document that outlines the principles and objectives of regional cooperation within the SAARC region.

questions.q: SAARC has been criticized for its slow pace of progress in implementing its decisions. Explain the reasons for this criticism. (10 marks)
questions.answer: Reasons for criticism include lack of trust among member states, limited institutional capacity, and lack of commitment to regional cooperation.

questions.q: What are the major areas of cooperation under the SAARC framework? Explain any two of them. (6 marks)
questions.answer: Major areas of cooperation include trade, tourism, and environment.

questions.q: Is SAARC a success in promoting regional cooperation in South Asia? Give reasons for your answer. (10 marks)
questions.answer: SAARC has had some success in promoting regional cooperation, but it has been limited by various challenges and obstacles.
